union before an Endorsement of their
position will be allowed.


In striking so suddenly the plumb
ers, it is declared, disobeyed one of the
first rules of the Trades Council,
which provides that all contemplated
raises in wages, or strikes, must first
be submitted. So far as is known by
union men of the city, this was not
done.


The plumbers union today claims to
have information which leads them to
believe that small force of non-union
plumbers have been shipped from
Minneapolis with this city as their des-
tination.


President Evans of the Master
Plumbers association was asked as to
the truth of the rumor, and stated he
knew nothing of the matter. How
ever, he would neither affirm nor deny
the report.


1t now looks as though an appalling
state of affairs confronts Spokane. If
the plumbers remain on strike much
longer all the building operations in
the city will be tied up and the build
ing trades thrown out of work.
